title: ares — Architecture
text: Initialize basis B intercept column. Fit OLS, compute residual r = y − fitted. Build orthonormal basis Q current B via modified Gram–Schmidt (build_Q helper, -place, returns rank Mq). Enumerate (parent, variable) pairs respecting interaction-degree cap. Dispatch RcppParallel::parallelFor(0, npairs, worker). worker scans eligible knots assigned (parent, var) slice computes joint RSS reduction hinge pair via Gram–Schmidt residual projection Q. Reduce: pick (parent, var, knot) maximum reduction. Tie-break smaller variable, smaller knot, smaller parent. Add hinge pair (s = +1, s = −1) new basis columns. Refit OLS via -place Householder QR (hand-rolled ols_qr); update RSS residual. relative RSS improvement < thresh, stop.

title: ares — Architecture
text: Sequential subset selection. step, drop term whose removal causes smallest RSS increase (subject keeping intercept). Compute GCV subset size: RSS / (n * (1 - (M + penalty*(M-1)/2)/n)^2). Select subset minimizing GCV. Refit OLS selected basis obtain final coefficients.

title: ares — Architecture
text: Uses RcppParallel TBB (parallelFor). Worker signature takes raw const double* pointers — Rcpp::* API calls. worker writes slot (false sharing). nthreads <= 1, dispatch site short-circuits serial loop, bypassing TBB scheduler overhead.

title: ares — Architecture
text: arithmetic double. QR hand-rolled (Householder) — LAPACK dependency, eliminates BLAS/LAPACK linker complexity. Rank guards: candidate columns whose perpendicular norm 1e3 * eps * scale skipped. Residual computed full OLS step (residual-update tricks); trades speed numerical robustness v0.0.0.9000.

title: ares — Architecture

title: ares — Architecture
text: C++ entry mars_fit_cpp calls RcppParallel::parallelFor flattening Rcpp objects std::vector<double> std::vector<int>. Workers read flattened buffers via const double* / const int* pointers write per-pair output slot. RcppParallel::setThreadOptions(numThreads = ...) called R-side ares.default() entering C++; C++ code observes count via nthreads parameter choose serial vs parallel paths.

title: ares — Architecture
text: Forward pass dominates runtime. Per-step cost: O(n × M²) Q construction + O(npairs × (n + K) × M_q) knot scoring (Friedman fast-LS prefix sums), K = #eligible knots per pair (typically ~50), M_q = rank Q (typically ≤ M). Friedman-1 n=1500 deg=2: ares-1t ~1.1s (~22s v0.0.0.9000), earth ~0.05s. Median single-thread ratio vs earth across inst/sims grid: ~28× (~152×). remaining wall-clock gap now dominated serial parts (build_Q, ols_qr) rather per-knot inner loop. Parallel scaling shrunk reason v0.2 roadmap item.

title: ares — Architecture
text: BLAS/LAPACK linkage: hand-rolled QR sufficient M ≤ 200 basis-term scale typical MARS. also dodges system-config issues (dev machine broken MKL config wouldn’t link). Selected.terms 1-indexed: returned R 1-based indices consistency earth’s convention. Term labels: (Intercept), h(x-cut), h(cut-x), products joined * interactions — matches earth’s labeling. Drop constant columns: emit single warning(), drop, continue. global state: .onLoad -op. Thread count set per-call via setThreadOptions. package-level options.

Forward + backward consume weights (KnotScanner prefix sums QR/downdate work transformed space). GCV denom uses normalised w (mean=1, n_eff=n). PI = yhat +/- qt(1 - alpha/2, df) * sigma_hat. - “lm”: fit |resid| ~ yhat closed-form (weighted closed form weights supplied) apply sqrt(pi/2) convert mean-absolute-deviation back sigma estimate. Captures simple heteroscedasticity. - predict.ares(interval = "pint", level = 0.95) returns matrix columns c(“fit”, “lwr”, “upr”). Errors loudly fit built without varmod, family != “gaussian”. title: ares: Fast MARS in roadrunner
text: ares() (shipped roadrunner package) fits Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines (MARS) models — flexible nonparametric regression technique introduced Friedman (1991). MARS forward pass adds basis functions form max(0, ±(x - knot)) (called hinges) one pair time, scaled parent term allow interactions. backward pass prunes terms minimizing GCV criterion. ares() mirrors API earth gaussian-core designed numerical parity earth taking advantage multi-core CPUs via RcppParallel.

title: ares: Fast MARS in roadrunner
text: earth package available, can directly compare fits. relative RSS difference typically fraction percent n >= 1000 — two implementations agree numerically even pick slightly different knot sets.
code:
if (requireNamespace("earth", quietly = TRUE)) {   set.seed(20260509)   n <- 200; p <- 5   x <- matrix(runif(n * p), n, p)   y <- 10 * sin(pi * x[, 1] * x[, 2]) + 20 * (x[, 3] - 0.5)^2 +        10 * x[, 4] + 5 * x[, 5] + rnorm(n)   fa <- ares(x, y, degree = 2, nthreads = 2)   fe <- earth::earth(x, y, degree = 2)   cat("ares  RSS:", round(fa$rss, 3), " GCV:", round(fa$gcv, 3),       " nterms:", length(fa$selected.terms), "
")   cat("earth RSS:", round(fe$rss, 3), " GCV:", round(fe$gcv, 3),       " nterms:", length(fe$selected.terms), "
")   cat("relative RSS difference:",       round(abs(fa$rss - fe$rss) / fe$rss, 4), "
") } #> ares  RSS: 222.61  GCV: 1.707  nterms: 16  #> earth RSS: 204.18  GCV: 1.518  nterms: 15  #> relative RSS difference: 0.0903

title: ares: Fast MARS in roadrunner
text: ares parallel-deterministic construction: changing nthreads change output.
code: set.seed(42) n <- 150; p <- 5 x <- matrix(runif(n * p), n, p) y <- 5 * pmax(0, x[, 1] - 0.5) * pmax(0, x[, 2] - 0.3) + rnorm(n) f1 <- ares(x, y, degree = 2, nthreads = 1) f2 <- ares(x, y, degree = 2, nthreads = 2) cat("identical selected.terms:", isTRUE(all.equal(f1$selected.terms, f2$selected.terms)), "\n") #> identical selected.terms: TRUE cat("max coef diff:           ", max(abs(f1$coefficients - f2$coefficients)), "\n") #> max coef diff:            0

title: ares: Fast MARS in roadrunner
text: ares v0.0.0.9000 currently slower absolute wall-clock earth — documented limitation headline target v0.1. Internally, ares uses O(n) per-knot scoring loop earth uses Friedman’s O(1)-per-knot Givens fast-LS update. Givens implementation roadmap. ares v0.0.0.9000 already gets right: Numerical correctness — fits track earth ~1% RSS across smoke grid. Threading determinism — bit-identical fits regardless thread count. Parallel scaling — 2-thread consistently ~1.5–1.8× faster 1-thread (RcppParallel worker real work; constant factor lives inside inner loop, threading layer). See inst/sims/ directory Monte Carlo benchmark scripts generated headline numbers README.md.

title: Brain Contributions — ares v0.0.0.9001 through v0.0.0.9020
text: MARS forward-pass inner loop scores candidate knot computing projections hinge-pair columns onto current basis. naive approach costs O(n * Mq) per knot; using prefix sums rows sorted candidate predictor reduces per-pair cost O(K * n * Mq) O((n + K) * Mq), K number candidate knots per (parent, variable) pair.

title: Brain Contributions — ares v0.0.0.9001 through v0.0.0.9020
text: (parent, variable) pair, rows sorted ascending x_j. Five scalar prefix-sum accumulators two length-Mq vector accumulators maintained: p = parent column value, x = predictor value, r = residual, Q = columns current orthonormal basis factor. Totals eligible rows (T_) precomputed one O(n  Mq) pass. knot position k (threshold t = x_k), high-side sums recovered : H_* = T_* - S_* - (contribution row k) key per-knot quantities follow O(Mq): RSS reduction adding hinge pair computed via 2-step Gram-Schmidt: add h+ first, project h- orthogonal h+ (already orthogonal span(Q)). reduction formula : Note h+ h- disjoint support (h+() > 0 iff x_i > t, h-() > 0 iff x_i < t), (Q’ h+)_q (Q’ h-)_q capture bulk cross-term Q-projections, direct inner products.

code: // Incremental Q1 append // Q1: n x current_M (stored column-major) // R:  current_M x current_M (upper triangular) // Qty: length current_M  void append_column(const double* c, int n, int M,                    double* Q1, double* R, double* Qty,                    double* resid, double& rss) {   std::vector<double> u(M);   for (int q = 0; q < M; ++q) {     double dot = 0;     for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) dot += Q1[i + q*n] * c[i];     u[q] = dot;   }   std::vector<double> c_perp(c, c + n);   for (int q = 0; q < M; ++q)     for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i)       c_perp[i] -= u[q] * Q1[i + q*n];   double d = 0;   for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) d += c_perp[i] * c_perp[i];   d = std::sqrt(d);   if (d < eps_rank) { /* rank-deficient: zero column */ return; }   for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) Q1[i + M*n] = c_perp[i] / d;   for (int q = 0; q < M; ++q) R[q + M*(M+1)] = u[q];   R[M + M*(M+1)] = d;   double qty_M = 0;   for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) qty_M += Q1[i + M*n] * resid[i] + 0; // resid is y - Q1*Qty   // Actually: qty_M = Q1[:,M]' * y (precomputed from c_perp' * y before normalising)   Qty[M] = qty_M;   for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) resid[i] -= Q1[i + M*n] * qty_M;   rss -= qty_M * qty_M; }

title: Brain Contributions — ares v0.0.0.9001 through v0.0.0.9020
text: Protocol: Fit central model (autotune desired) full training data. Record chosen hyperparameters (degree, penalty, nk, fast_k). Draw row-bootstrap sample (replacement, n rows). Fit model using central fit’s hyperparameters (re-tuning). Store fitted object list. Compute predictions central fit n_boot replicates. Return mean point estimate. Return SD across (n_boot + 1) predictions se (accessible via se.fit = TRUE predict). Composition autotune: - Autotune runs central fit. - bootstrap replicate refits tuned params. Cost (n_boot + 1) * single_fit_cost, n_boot * autotune_cost. - makes autotune + bagging practical: n_boot = 50 ~0.5s fit, total cost ~25s regardless autotune complexity. RNG separation: - reproducibility seed set, use two separate streams: one CV fold partitioning (seed S), one bootstrap draws (seed S + constant). ensures () streams reproducible fixed seed (b) changing fold partition perturb bootstrap sequence vice versa. - Save restore caller’s .Random.seed around internal RNG use user’s global RNG stream unaffected.

title: Fast Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines — ares
text: x numeric matrix data frame predictors, model formula calling formula method. ... Additional arguments. Currently ignored. data Used formula method . data frame. y numeric response vector. Length must equal nrow(x). degree Maximum interaction degree. Default 1. nk Maximum number basis terms forward pass. Default min(200, max(20, 2 * ncol(x))) + 1. penalty GCV penalty. Default (degree > 1) 3 else 2. thresh Forward-pass relative-RSS early-stop threshold. Default 0.001. minspan Minimum knot span. 0 (default) selects automatic value. endspan Knot offset data ends. 0 (default) selects automatic. adjust.endspan Multiplier applied endspan candidate hinge deepen existing interaction (parent term degree least 1). Default 1 gaussian / poisson / gamma, 2 binomial (auto-flipped v0.0.0.9026 /B-test justification auto.linpreds). Pass 1 2 explicitly override family default. auto.linpreds TRUE, best forward-pass knot candidate hinge sits boundary variable's eligible range, substitutes linear term (dirs = 2) hinge pair. Default FALSE gaussian / poisson / gamma, TRUE binomial (auto- flipped v0.0.0.9026 based inst/sims/v0.25 /B test: earth- like settings tie improve AUC mlbench binary cells, regress gaussian regression cells). Pass explicit value override family default. fast.k Fast-MARS priority cache size (Friedman 1993). forward pass always rescores pairs whose parent added previous step; top fast.k remaining stale pairs (ranked age-discounted cached score) also rescored, rest contribute cached reduction. 0 disables cache (every pair rescored every step). Default 10. fast.beta Age-penalty fast-MARS priority cache: stale pair's effective score cached_rss_red / (1 + fast.beta * age). Default 1.0. matters fast.k > 0. nprune Maximum number terms backward pruning (default nk). pmethod Pruning method: "backward" (default), "none", "cv" K-fold cross-validated subset selection. "cv" requires nfold > 0 (implicitly sets nfold = 5 specified). nfold Number CV folds (default 0 = CV; GCV-based backward selection). nfold > 0, pmethod promoted "cv". ncross Number CV repetitions nfold > 0. repetition builds fresh fold partition; per-size mean MSE averaged across nfold * ncross evaluations. Default 1. stratify TRUE (default), folds quantile-stratified y fold similar response distribution. Useful small n / skewed responses; cheap. seed.cv Optional integer seed fold partitioning. NULL (default) uses current RNG state; pass integer reproducible CV partitions. cv.1se TRUE, applies one-standard-error rule pmethod="cv": among sizes whose mean CV-MSE within one standard error minimum, pick smallest. Trades bit accuracy parsimony. Default FALSE (pick argmin mean CV-MSE, .e. size minimises holdout error directly). autotune TRUE, runs inner cross-validated grid search (degree, penalty) refits winner. grid degree {1, 2} (extended {1, 2, 3} nk large enough) crossed penalty {0.5*d, 1.0*d, 2.0*d, 3.0*d} degree d. Inner CV uses nfold (default 5 autotune nfold == 0) respects seed.cv. chosen (degree, penalty) full grid CV-MSE returned $autotune. Default FALSE (current grf-style fast default path). Subsequent versions extend grid (v0.16: nk, v0.17: autotune.speed, v0.18: n.boot). autotune.speed One "balanced" (default), "quality", "fast". meaningful autotune = TRUE. "quality": forces fast.k = 0 every cell (fast-MARS priority cache; every (parent, var) pair rescored every step). Matches v0.10 quality v0.10 wall-clock cost. "fast": forces fast.k = 5. aggressive cache; cheapest. "balanced" (default): sweeps fast.k {10, 25, Inf} (Inf means "cache") inside autotune grid picks smallest fast.k whose mean CV-MSE within 1% best. Trades marginal accuracy hit marginal speed gain. autotune.warmstart TRUE (default; meaningful autotune = TRUE) n >= 200, ares first runs autotune 15% subsample (capped 200 rows). subsample decisive winner – best-per-degree CV-MSE 5% next-best degree's best cell – (degree, penalty, nk, fast.k) used directly refit full data, skipping full-data grid. Cuts autotune wall-clock ~5x well-separated DGPs. n.boot Number bootstrap replicate fits bagging. Default 0 (bagging). > 0, result holds list n.boot ares fits $boot$fits, fit row-bootstrap sample data. augmented predict() averages predictions across replicates plus central fit reports per-prediction standard deviation attr(predictions, "sd"). Bagging composes autotune – replicate fits central fit's chosen (degree, penalty, nk, fast.k) cost (n.boot + 1) * fit_cost, autotune grid times n.boot. na.action Strategy missing values x. Either "impute" (default) "omit". "impute" replaces column's NAs column's median (computed non-missing rows) stores medians fit predict() can reapply future newdata. "omit" drops every row NA x. actions emit warning summarising affected rows / columns. Missing values y always hard error – sensible way impute regression target. NaN +/-Inf x also rejected outright regardless na.action. family Response family. One "gaussian" (default; numeric y, identity link, OLS coefficients), "binomial" (binary y, logit link), "poisson" (non-negative integer-valued y, log link), "gamma" (strictly positive y, log link). non-gaussian families forward backward passes still run original numeric y gaussian (matching earth\'s GLM strategy); backward pruning selected basis refit response via stats::glm.fit() appropriate family object, $coefficients, $fitted.values, $linear.predictor come GLM. Bagging, CV pruning, autotune, weights compose GLM families; inner model-selection still uses (weighted) Gaussian MSE latent scale (cheap adequate term selection — final coefficients use IRLS). weights Optional non-negative numeric vector length nrow(x) weighted least squares fitting. Default NULL (unweighted OLS). supplied, engine implements WLS via sqrt(weights) transform design response, forward + backward consume weights (knot selection, GCV pruning, CV pruning, autotune weight-aware). Weights renormalised internally mean(weights) = 1; keeps GCV denominator valid ("effective sample size" equals n). NA / NaN / negative weights rejected. weights = rep(1, n) fit byte-identical unweighted path. varmod Variance-model strategy used predict.ares() build prediction intervals. One : "none" (default): variance model stored. predict() returns conditional mean; interval = "pint" unavailable. "const": store single training residual standard deviation sigma_hat = sqrt(weighted_RSS / df_residual). PIs yhat +/- qt(0.975, df) * sigma_hat. "lm": fit small linear model |resid| ~ yhat training fit use prediction (multiplied sqrt(pi/2) estimates sigma) new yhat. Captures simple heteroscedasticity. meaningful family = "gaussian". binomial / poisson / gamma variance mean-determined; argument silently ignored (PIs require family-specific intervals implemented — see ?stats::predict.glm ). Defaults "none" (SEs default). trace Trace level. 0 = silent (default), 1 = forward-pass progress. nthreads Number threads. 0 (default) selects RcppParallel::defaultNumThreads().
